canonicalization_policy.duplicate_rulespec_citation: Select the newest snapshot as canonical; retain every snapshot byte and hash.
canonicalization_policy.existing_active_citation: Keep active axiom-corpus text canonical; attach every RuleSpec body and URL as structured provenance.
canonicalization_policy.existing_inactive_citation: Promote the newest axiom-corpus snapshot as canonical; attach every RuleSpec body and URL as structured provenance.
canonicalization_policy.rulespec_only_citation: Promote the RuleSpec snapshot without changing its body.
canonicalization_policy.url_union: Keep one canonical source_url and retain the exact sorted URL set in metadata.source_urls.

source_retention_policy.active_artifacts: Tracked normalized JSONL snapshots are the complete resolver-visible source for every promoted body and structured URL.
source_retention_policy.fallbacks: None. The active selector reads only tracked axiom-corpus JSONL/PDF artifacts.
source_retention_policy.new_official_ingests: New legislation.gov.uk sections retain both tracked normalized JSONL and deterministic gzip containers of the exact CLML bytes. The inventory and signed ingest manifest bind the container hashes; migration provenance and tests decompress each container and bind the original XML hashes.
source_retention_policy.original_official_bytes: Existing RuleSpec source XML remains immutable at the pinned rulespec-uk commit and is addressed by repository path and SHA-256. The two official-source ingests absent from RuleSpec are tracked here as deterministic gzip containers so their upstream commentary key identifiers do not trigger push-protection false positives while their exact XML bytes remain locally reconstructible and hash-verifiable.
